title: Lead Gameplay AI Engineer
company_focus:
Independent AAA game development studio focused on
narrative-driven experiences
description:
That's No Moon is an independent studio of storytellers and
game-makers who believe in the power of narrative-driven
experiences. With a diverse team of talented developers, our
shared vision is to create the future of AAA experiences
with a culture of creativity, collaboration, and responsible
production practices. Our passionate team of developers
helps shape our studio, our projects, and our future.

Gameplay AI is a core component of video games used to tell
an engaging and exciting story through gameplay. We are
looking for a Lead Gameplay AI Engineer who is excited to
push the boundaries of Gameplay AI in video games for our
forthcoming AAA title. This role will work side-by-side with
designers and provide creative input to craft believable,
innovative AI experiences. In this role, you will architect,
build and maintain a variety of AI systems that bring
characters and the world to life in service of outstanding,
memorable gameplay.

Responsibilities:

- Own gameplay AI systems and related engineering tasking and
  development.
- Directly manage a team of AI engineers, including career
  management, growth, and mentorship.
- Architect, implement, and maintain production quality C/C++
  for AI systems, behaviors, tools, and workflows.
- Give technical leadership and counsel, ranging from short-
  term AI problem solving to long-term AI roadmap planning.
- Provide the production team with guidance for scheduling,
  while identifying and reducing risks across AI feature
  development.
- Collaborate with engineers and other disciplines to provide
  holistic solutions with an emphasis on delivering
  exceptional gameplay experiences.
- Build and foster a highly collaborative, transparent culture
  that is safe, and values innovation, diversity, and work-
  life balance.
- Drive innovation in AI character behaviors, ensuring NPCs
  feel believable and contribute meaningfully to gameplay
  and storytelling.
- Analyze different approaches to solve AI problems and
  discuss the tradeoffs between them.
- Write and maintain relevant documentation.

Requirements & Skills:

- Shipped at least one AAA game or equivalent complexity in a
  Gameplay AI-related role.
- Shipped at least one AAA game or equivalent in complexity
  with Unreal Engine
- At least 7 years of professional C++ experience.
- Strong math skills.
- Console development experience is a plus
- Experience with machine learning is a plus.

Qualities:

- Ability to lead and mentor engineers, guiding growth in both
  AI-specific and broader technical skills.
- Excellent interpersonal skills, communication, both verbally
  and written.
- Work independently with minimal supervision.
- Good conflict resolution abilities across varied
  disciplines.

TNM considers a number of factors when determining each
role's base pay range, including industry benchmarks,
location, experience, and other job specific skills. In
addition to base pay, this role may be eligible for other
incentive compensation including equity and profit share.
The estimated base pay range for this role is U.S. only and
not applicable for locations outside of the U.S.

The estimated base pay range for this role is: $195,000 -
$243,000

TNM offers competitive and comprehensive benefits that
include medical, dental, vision, 401(k) match, flexible PTO,
supplemental life and disability, and more. Eligibility to
participate in these benefits may vary based on country of
residence and/or part-time or temporary employment status.

That's No Moon is an equal opportunity employer and all
qualified applicants will receive consideration for
employment without regard to race, ancestry, color,
citizenship, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender
identity or expression, marital status, pregnancy, age,
national origin, disability status, legally protected
physical or mental disability, genetic information,
protected veteran status, or any other characteristic
protected by law.

To those interested in submitting for roles at That's No
Moon, we have a few reminders to ensure a safe application
process.

1. If one of our recruiters reaches out to you, it will be
with a "@thatsnomoon.com" email address. We do not email via
personal email addresses either. 2. We do not conduct
interviews via Discord or WhatsApp. 3. We will not ask you
to provide sensitive personal information. 4. We will never
request that you buy equipment specifically for your
interview/test.

We are saddened that people are being taken advantage of and
ask that, if you ever have any doubts about whether you are
speaking to an official member of the That's No Moon team,
to reach out to our official social handles directly.

responsibilities: Own gameplay AI systems and related engineering tasking and development
Directly manage a team of AI engineers including career management, growth, and mentorship
Architect, implement, and maintain production quality C/C++ for AI systems, behaviors, tools, and workflows
Provide technical leadership ranging from short-term AI problem solving to long-term AI roadmap planning

use_cases: NPC behavior and decision-making systems for AAA video games
Character AI that creates believable and memorable gameplay experiences
AI-driven storytelling and narrative gameplay mechanics
Game world AI systems that bring environments and characters to life
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role is focused on building AI systems for gameplay —
the engineer directly architects, implements, and maintains
AI behaviors, NPC systems, and related tools. While this is
game AI (behavior trees, state machines, pathfinding) rather
than ML/AI, the role involves hands-on creation of AI
systems, making it ai-first. ML experience is listed only as
a plus, not a core requirement.
